The radio in our house was tuned to the same station every morning. My father would not tolerate it being changed before nine.
setup

setup

There were two mango trees at the back of the property. Every summer they produced more than we could eat and we gave the rest to the neighbours.
setup

My mother and her sisters spoke in a mix of Nepali and Newari that I never fully learned. They switched without thinking.

The clinic was half a day's walk. For small things we waited to see if they passed. Most things passed.

My husband was drinking heavily that year. I did not tell anyone in my family. I kept managing and kept quiet.
tension

tension

My parents could not agree on where we would live after my father retired. The argument lasted the better part of a year.

We waited three weeks for news of my uncle after the earthquake. The phone lines were down and no one could reach the village.

My daughter stopped eating properly the year she was thirteen. I did not know what to do and the doctors did not seem to either.
tension

The old man from next door came to the hospital and sat with us all night without being asked. He brought nothing, said almost nothing, and it was exactly right.

I told my father the truth about what had happened, finally, after keeping it from him for two years. He was quiet for a long time. Then he said he already knew.
